summary: Small build-tooling PR that prevented macOS builds from selecting the Linux-oriented wibo wrapper. The PR body shows macOS was attempting to run build/tools/wibo and failing with "cannot execute binary file"; the diff changes tools/project.py so use_wibo() only returns true on Linux, not Darwin. The PR was closed without a recorded merge and has no review discussion in the provided slice.

key_files.reason: Changed Project.use_wibo platform gate so wibo is only selected on sys.platform == "linux".

what_changed: Updated tools/project.py in Project.use_wibo from allowing sys.platform == "linux" or "darwin" to allowing only sys.platform == "linux".
Kept the existing conditions for wibo_tag presence, supported machine architectures, and absence of an explicit wrapper.
The intended effect is that macOS/Darwin no longer tries to execute build/tools/wibo during compiler invocation; the PR body describes the desired path as using wine instead of wibo.
No C source, headers, symbols, or matching output changed; this was a one-line build tooling adjustment.
smart_moves: Fixed the platform-selection predicate at the source of the wrapper decision rather than adding a macOS-specific workaround at call sites.
Made the smallest possible diff: one boolean condition changed, reducing risk to unrelated build behavior.
Preserved architecture checks and explicit-wrapper override behavior, so Linux wibo usage and user-provided wrapper configuration remained untouched.
Used the concrete macOS failure mode from the build log to justify the platform gate.
decomp_lessons: Build wrapper selection must account for both OS and CPU architecture; an available architecture string such as x86_64 is not enough to prove a helper binary is executable on that platform.
For decompilation projects with custom compiler wrappers, prefer centralizing platform policy in a single helper such as use_wibo() so future ports can be fixed with minimal churn.
MacOS build regressions can block progress checks even when source matching is unaffected, so build-tooling compatibility is part of maintaining decomp velocity.
When documenting tooling fixes, include before/after command output; the failure log here made the impact and validation clear.
naming_conventions: The boolean helper is named use_wibo(), matching its role as a predicate for whether the wibo wrapper should be selected.
The optional wrapper-related fields visible in the diff use concise descriptive names: wibo_tag and wrapper.
No new decomp symbols, structs, headers, or gameplay naming conventions were introduced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or C matching tactics were involved.
The practical matching-support tactic was to keep macOS builds operational so progress generation and object builds can complete.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews are present in the provided PR slice.
The PR was closed with merged_at null, so the slice does not prove that this exact patch was merged.
follow_up_queries: Find later or alternate PRs that merged the macOS wibo/wine platform gate, since PR 2417 is closed with no merged_at value.
Search tools/project.py history for further wrapper-selection changes involving wibo, wine, Darwin, or Apple Silicon.
Check whether macOS CI or documentation was updated to clarify required wrapper behavior for Metrowerks compiler execution.
